mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation support and short-horizon risk appetite, so lower policy rates predict higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising faster than the federal funds rate, real policy rates compress and the market should reprice toward tighter future monetary conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the interaction signal loses sign consistency across rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating UNRATE rise signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income expectations, and lower forward equity demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in a majority of labor-deterioration triggers, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO growth indicates better industrial supply-side momentum and stronger expected earnings for cyclicals, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 5-day SPX returns are not more frequent when INDPRO accelerates on a 1-month basis, or if the 1-year rolling hit rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates improving domestic demand that lifts revenue expectations and housing-sensitive consumption, so the S&P 500 should post positive returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST are both above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-above-average condition does not outperform each single proxy alone, or if triggered 60-day returns are not statistically greater than zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Falling PAYEMS confirms payroll contraction and weaker earnings capacity, so a negative employment impulse should precede lower S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns remain positive after PAYEMS declines on a 1-month basis, or if the signal has a negative Sharpe ratio over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDPC1 growth and RSAFS indicates weakening final demand and lower corporate revenue visibility, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is negative while RSAFS is below its 6-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weakness condition is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the combined signal adds no lift over GDPC1 alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a low but rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with stubborn CPIAUCSL inflation signals policy pressure on multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if, during tightening cycles, 20-day SPX returns are not lower when inflation stays elevated relative to policy rates, or if the signal reverses sign in consecutive tightening episodes.
